titleStmt.title: Letter from Peter Mooney to his sister Katie Mooney, 29 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Peter Mooney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
May 29th 1916
Dear Katie,
I am sure you were expecting a letter from
me this long time. I had not the time for writing
as we were three days on the march + a weary time
it was too. I hope you did not send me any parcels
lately if you did they will get lost. At present I
am attached to no Divisions so do not write until you
hear from me again. We are having a rest first now
but I would rather be attached to some Division as time
hangs very heavy on me & I have no money I hope we
will not be here long. I keep in the very best of health
& we ate getting plenty to eat & the weather is lovely
& fine. I wrote to Paddy first before we started on
the march I hope he recieved it alright. I hope that
Sinn Fein Crowd are entirely wiped out of Ireland
now. They know by this time the strength of England &
so will the huns when we start. I hope all
are well in the Paddock remember me to them all.
I think I could sleep out in the garden at home now
I have slept in a lot worse places out here. I have
no more to say at present I will write soon again.
I now say Good Bye & May God Bless & keep you all.
Good Bye. Peter Mooney
You might send me a Postal Order
for 6 ./- as I might be here for some
time register it I may get it
sometime. My address
Pr. P. Mooney 101267: R.F.A.
No 6 Large Rest Camp
B. E. Fi France.
Miss. K Mooney
Caulstown
Dunboyne
Co. Meath
Ireland.
